description: Tablet fragmentation observed — tablets breaking apart inside blister pack before removal. Multiple blisters affected in same carton.
Tablet surface shows visible cracks. Patient noticed before taking dose. Tablets from multiple strips in same box affected.
Black foreign particle found inside sealed film-coated tablet bottle. Consumer submitted product sample. Particle appears to be a metal fragment.
Expiry date absent from outer carton. Date is present on inner bottle but not printed on carton. Multiple units from same batch confirmed by distributor.
Capsule shell discoloration — capsules appear brownish instead of white/pink. Patient concerned about product integrity.
Tablets sticking together — multiple tablets fused inside sealed bottle. Patient reported difficulty separating tablets without breaking them.
Hospital pharmacy found foreign white tablet mixed in with methotrexate tablets. Drug appears to be a different product. Oncology patient — serious safety concern. Batch immediately quarantined.
French language patient information leaflet missing from EU multilingual pack. English and German present but French required for French market distribution.
Low potency suspected — patient reports reduced therapeutic effect. Hospital lab assay shows 87% vs specification of 95-105%. Batch number not recorded at time of dispensing.
Visible glass particles in vincristine injection vial detected by nurse before administration. Paediatric oncology patient. Critical — did not administer. Batch hold requested immediately.
